Case Narrative

In brief, for the investigating officer:

  • A total of Rs 1,668,323.99 moved across the accounts in this case, with Rs 301,132.00 coming from outside the suspect network.
  • HARI sent out Rs 1,029,941.00, which is the largest amount of funds in this case.
  • VIKRAM SAXENA spread money to 79 different accounts.
  • VIKRAM SAXENA collected money from 47 different accounts.
  • Rs 40,350,591.00 of incoming funds was used for transfers or withdrawals, and Rs 205,099.00 could not be fully traced.
  • The account to investigate first is PRIME DISTRIBUTORS ASSOCIATION, which shows the strongest signs of suspicious behavior in this case.

4 · Findings by Account

PRIME DISTRIBUTORS ASSOCIATION strong
Account Number 18306700003 · IDFC First Bank · Score 882
Rank #1

The account of PRIME DISTRIBUTORS ASSOCIATION (18306700003) is suspicious due to its unusual money behavior, including a large credit of 1600000.00 and subsequent debits, as well as large transfers with no prior observed relationships. This behavior warrants further investigation.

  • Money Trail Tracing strong — A credit of 1600000.00 was traced into 5 subsequent debits, with the entire amount allocated and none remaining untraced, indicating a potentially suspicious money trail. (+7 more of this type)
  • First Contact Large Transfer strong — A large transfer of 500000.00 occurred between HARI (2248145316) and PRIME DISTRIBUTORS ASSOCIATION (18306700003) on 2025-04-05, with no prior observed relationship between the parties.
  • Failed Reversed Transaction Detection weak — A debit of 500000.0 was followed by a near-equal credit of 500000.0 with explicit reversal/reference evidence, suggesting a potential failed reversed transaction.
  • Money Flow Graph Construction structural — A money-flow graph was constructed with 167 nodes and 350 directed transaction edges, indicating a complex network of transactions involving PRIME DISTRIBUTORS ASSOCIATION (18306700003) and other entities.
  • Reversal Clusters weak — The account PRIME DISTRIBUTORS ASSOCIATION (18306700003) has 5 debit-reversal pairs, indicating systematic transaction reversal activity, with a reversal pair count exceeding the runtime threshold.
  • Round Value Debit Patterns weak — The account made 63 suspicious round-value debits totaling 14936000.00, with repeated amounts such as 200,000, 300,000, and 250,000, suggesting a potential pattern of round-value debit activity.

Case Overview Graph

The graph shows 7 clusters with the most notable cluster being cluster_003, which has a total score of 1218.0005. Cluster_001 has the most edges with 237 and the highest account count of 5. An investigator should take note of the large total scores in clusters cluster_003, cluster_004, and cluster_005, with scores of 1218.0005, 813.3992, and 831.2352 respectively.

Account Interconnection Graph

This static exhibit is filtered for legibility: it shows the material account-to-account relationships and only the most evidential external counterparties, while the full-density network remains available in the UI graph data.

Suspicious Activity Timeline

The graph shows a timeline of suspicious activity from 2019-11-26 to 2025-12-31, with a total of 12492 transactions, excluding 12 flagged transactions. The most notable figure is the large number of transactions over a nearly 6-year period. An investigator should take note of the sheer volume of transactions and the extended time span, which may indicate a complex and long-running scheme.

Fraud-Pattern Summary

The graph shows a summary of fraud patterns with the most notable figure being 35 instances of Money Trail Tracing, indicating a significant focus on tracing money flows. The busiest patterns also include 6 instances of Low Value Account Testing and Round Value Debit Patterns. An investigator should take note of these figures to understand the scope of potential fraudulent activities. The time span is not explicitly stated, but the data provides insight into the types of patterns detected.

Data Security & Method (Appendix)

What stays on this machine

  • All fraud detection, scoring and ranking run on this machine (deterministic code).
  • Raw bank statements and extracted transactions never leave the machine.
  • The money-flow graph, clusters and suspicion scores are computed locally.

What is sent to an external service

  • Only small, redacted text samples are sent to the language model to describe a statement's column layout when local parsing is insufficient.
  • For images / scanned PDFs, the page pixels are sent to a vision model (the one unavoidable case).
  • The language model only rewrites explanations into plain language; it never changes a number, account, or decision.
